title: athanasia quinquedentata subsp. rigens
intro: Athanasia quinquedentata subsp. rigens, also known as the Cape Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its bright orange or yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infections. The health benefits of Athanasia quinquedentata subsp. rigens include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
